children.name: Rhincodon
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the whale shark, the largest fish in the ocean, a gentle filter feeder that cruises tropical seas sieving plankton and small fish through its enormous gaping mouth.

children.name: Rhincodon typus
children.description: Rhincodon typus (Whale shark) is a whale shark species found in Africa and Asia and North America and South America and Oceania regions.

children.name: Ginglymostoma
children.description: The nurse sharks, two species of sluggish, bottom-dwelling sharks found in shallow tropical waters, often resting motionless on the seafloor and using their fleshy barbels to detect buried prey.

children.name: Ginglymostoma cirratum
children.description: Ginglymostoma cirratum (Nurse shark) is a nurse species found in Africa and North America and South America regions.

children.name: Ginglymostoma unami
children.namedAfter: Latin unami, 'one who is loved,' referencing the species' long confusion with G. cirratum
children.description: Ginglymostoma unami (Pacific nurse shark) is a nurse species found in North America and South America regions.

children.name: Nebrius
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the tawny nurse shark, a large nocturnal shark of Indo-Pacific reefs and sandy flats that can suction prey from crevices and occasionally hauls itself partially out of water.

children.name: Nebrius ferrugineus
children.description: Nebrius ferrugineus (Tawny nurse shark) is a nurse species found in Africa and Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Stegostoma
children.description: The zebra shark, a single species inhabiting Indo-Pacific coral reefs and sandy bottoms, named for the bold striped pattern of juveniles that fades to a spotted adult colouration.

children.name: Stegostoma fasciatum
children.description: Stegostoma fasciatum (Zebra shark) is a zebra species found in Africa and Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Orectolobus
children.description: The wobbegongs, a genus of ornately camouflaged ambush sharks with flattened bodies and elaborate dermal lobes that blend seamlessly with coral and rocky reef substrates in Australasian and western Pacific waters.

children.name: Orectolobus maculatus
children.description: Orectolobus maculatus (Spotted wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus ornatus
children.description: Orectolobus ornatus (Ornate wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus halei
children.description: Orectolobus halei (Gulf wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus wardi
children.description: Orectolobus wardi (Northern wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus parvimaculatus
children.description: Orectolobus parvimaculatus (Dwarf spotted wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus reticulatus
children.description: Orectolobus reticulatus (Network wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus leptolineatus
children.description: Orectolobus leptolineatus (Indonesian wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Asia region.

children.name: Orectolobus floridus
children.description: Orectolobus floridus (Floral banded wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus hutchinsi
children.description: Orectolobus hutchinsi (Western wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Orectolobus japonicus
children.description: Orectolobus japonicus (Japanese wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Asia region.

children.name: Eucrossorhinus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the tasselled wobbegong, a master of camouflage with highly branched dermal lobes around its head that create a fringe resembling seaweed, enabling it to ambush prey on coral reefs.

children.name: Eucrossorhinus dasypogon
children.description: Eucrossorhinus dasypogon (Tasselled wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Sutorectus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the cobbler wobbegong, a robust, flattened shark endemic to southern Australia with intricate skin patterning that provides exceptional camouflage on the seafloor.

children.name: Sutorectus tentaculatus
children.description: Sutorectus tentaculatus (Cobbler wobbegong) is a wobbegong species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Chiloscyllium
children.description: The bamboo sharks, eight small, slender species of the Indo-Pacific that can use their pectoral fins to 'walk' across the reef at low tide and can survive brief periods out of water.

children.name: Chiloscyllium punctatum
children.description: Chiloscyllium punctatum (Brown-banded bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Chiloscyllium plagiosum
children.description: Chiloscyllium plagiosum (White-spotted bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Chiloscyllium griseum
children.description: Chiloscyllium griseum (Grey bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Chiloscyllium indicum
children.description: Chiloscyllium indicum (Slender bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Chiloscyllium hasseltii
children.description: Chiloscyllium hasseltii (Hasselt's bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Chiloscyllium burmense
children.description: Chiloscyllium burmense (Burmese bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Chiloscyllium caeruleopunctatum
children.description: Chiloscyllium caeruleopunctatum (Bluespotted bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Africa region.

children.name: Chiloscyllium arabicum
children.description: Chiloscyllium arabicum (Arabian bamboo shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium
children.description: The epaulette sharks, a genus of nine slender, long-tailed species found in shallow Indo-Pacific waters, named for the conspicuous ocellated spot behind the pectoral fin that resembles a military epaulette.

children.name: Hemiscyllium ocellatum
children.description: Hemiscyllium ocellatum (Epaulette shark) is a bamboo species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium freycineti
children.description: Hemiscyllium freycineti (Indonesian speckled carpetshark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium galei
children.description: Hemiscyllium galei (Gale's epaulette shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium hallstromi
children.description: Hemiscyllium hallstromi (Papuan epaulette shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium henryi
children.description: Hemiscyllium henryi (Henry's epaulette shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium michaeli
children.description: Hemiscyllium michaeli (Michael's epaulette shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium strahani
children.description: Hemiscyllium strahani (Hooded carpetshark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium trispeculare
children.description: Hemiscyllium trispeculare (Speckled carpetshark) is a bamboo species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Hemiscyllium halmahera
children.description: Hemiscyllium halmahera (Halmahera epaulette shark) is a bamboo species found in Asia region.

children.name: Brachaelurus
children.description: The blind sharks, two species endemic to shallow coastal waters of eastern and northern Australia, named for their habit of retracting the nictitating membrane as they are pulled from the water, giving the appearance of blindness.

children.name: Brachaelurus waddi
children.description: Brachaelurus waddi (Blind shark) is a blind species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Brachaelurus colcloughi
children.description: Brachaelurus colcloughi (Bluegrey carpetshark) is a blind species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Parascyllium
children.description: The collared carpetsharks, a genus of five to seven elongated species endemic to Australian waters, distinguished by the distinctive collar-like marking encircling the throat and a slender, eel-like body.

children.name: Parascyllium variolatum
children.description: Parascyllium variolatum (Necklace carpetshark) is a collared species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Parascyllium collare
children.description: Parascyllium collare (Collared carpetshark) is a collared species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Parascyllium elongatum
children.description: Parascyllium elongatum (Elongate carpetshark) is a collared species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Parascyllium sparsimaculatum
children.description: Parascyllium sparsimaculatum (Ginger carpetshark) is a collared species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Parascyllium ferrugineum
children.description: Parascyllium ferrugineum (Rusty carpetshark) is a collared species found in Oceania region.

children.name: Cirrhoscyllium
children.description: The barbelthroat carpetsharks, a genus of three rare, deep-reef sharks found only in the northwest Pacific, easily recognised by the pair of barbels hanging from the throat region.

children.name: Cirrhoscyllium expolitum
children.description: Cirrhoscyllium expolitum (Barbelthroat carpetshark) is a collared species found in Asia region.

children.name: Cirrhoscyllium formosanum
children.description: Cirrhoscyllium formosanum (Taiwan saddled carpetshark) is a collared species found in Asia region.

children.name: Cirrhoscyllium japonicum
children.description: Cirrhoscyllium japonicum (Saddle carpetshark) is a collared species found in Asia region.

children.name: Pseudoginglymostoma
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the short-tail nurse shark, a small, little-known species restricted to the western Indian Ocean with a distinctive short, blunt snout and a rounded caudal fin.

children.name: Pseudoginglymostoma brevicaudatum
children.description: Pseudoginglymostoma brevicaudatum (Short-tail nurse shark) is a nurse species found in Africa region.
